msItem.author: Stephen of Whitby
msItem.title: About the foundation of St Mary's Abbey, York; includes list of abbots and brief annals until 1267

msItem.author: Thurstan, Archbishop of York
msItem.title: A copy of a letter from Thurstan describing the foundation of St. Mary's Abbey at Fountains by a colony from York in 1132

msItem.author: unknown
msItem.title: Annals of St Mary's Abbey York, 1258-1326

msItem.author: unknown
msItem.title: Excerpts from Hugo de Sancto Victore (f. 211r contains a list of Latin words beginning with P.)

history.origin: England, St Mary's Abbey York c 1312-1333, though endleaves ff. iv-ix are 13th century, and endleaves ff. 209-212 are 14th century.

provenance.p: Henry Jackson, 1586-1662. The inscription 'Henricus Jacksonus Oxoniensis Anno 1600' has been erased.
Apparently purchased by the Bodleian from Henry Jackson in 1619-20.

revisionDesc.change: Corrected line numbering errors (from 111 onward and 578 onward).
Punctuation complete and MS viewed in Oxford to check final queries. One note to complete on line 474.
First pass complete; wants punctuation and a final check against the MS in Oxford at a number of points.

body.note: A line appears to have been cut off from the top of the page, equivalent to T 18: Garce ou garzoun le deit sure.

lg.l: mangez le griuegrive au dinere·. feldfare

l.note: Markings above the word cervel suggest there may be a faded gloss here.
Markings above the word temples suggest a faded gloss.

body.note: A line appears to have been cut off from the top of the page, equivalent to T 51: En les pauperes sount les ciz

lg.l: Amounte les oez sunt les surcyles·.
Le nes deit aueravere par reisoun·
Vous auezavez la leuerlever &e le leuerlever·
La leuerlever si enclos les denz·,
Le leuerlever au boys se tyent dedenz;
La bouche par conceile du palete rofe
Vus dirra si vin seit bon &e net.
Le col, la gorge, le mentoun,
De Desouz la gorge est la furcele,The gloss is illegible due to damage.

VnUn os rounchefrounchéThe reading frounché is likely a slip for fourché, found in other manuscripts, unless it is to be considered an adjectival form of AND entry frunce: unevenness, irregularity. que fraunceis si apele.
Chose que checune seit bien dire:
Les espaules, les braces, la peitrine.
Mes iejeo vus fray la mounstresoun
Des choses que ne sunt pas commun.
bau leuerlevere &e la leuerlevere suzaine. nether lip·
oreyle si ad molet.The gloss is illegible due to damage.
&E les espaules ount les blasouns·. suld ban
De Desouz le brace est le essuile·,
De Desouz la charete ausi le essuyle.
De Desouz le monteyn surd le bruyl·. myst
Em base terre est bon soyle·.
Le vente de byse greuegreve le oyle·.
ApresAprés le auste cheste le foyle·.
La reisoun pur quey dire voyle:
Par les espaules issunt les bracez.
coutes, les maynes ne lerai pas.·
Si est trouevé le count de la mayn·;
La paume de dedenz, la glay dehors·.
Le poyne est dite qaunt main est clos·.
VnUn poynge est qaunt la mayn·
De rien est clos ou trouevé playn·.
Deuz mayns ensemble voides ou playnes

body.note: Trimming of the page has resulted in a line lost here, corresponding to MS C 138: Enmy le ventre si est le oumbyl.

lg.l: Les nages, les quicez, la forchure
En la iaumbejaumbe est la sure
Mes war la chanel de blesçure. scin ban
De denzDedenz le corps de checun
eEst trouevé faey, quere &e pomoun, liver lung
Vus auezavez la chare &e le char,
Jeo vey la chare sere en
La chare par hydoure en hom
ount Several lines have been lost here due to damage.

body.note: There appears to be at least one line missing due to trimming of the leaf.

lg.l: curray est enceynté·.Whereas other manuscripts here distinguish between ceinte and enceinte, the variant form enceynté used here fails to make sense of the passage's intention to disambiguate in order to avoid potential social embarrassment.
pomes pur ceo que il ayment·.

body.note: Several lines are missing due to damage at the bottom of the page.
There appears to be at least one line missing due to trimming of the leaf.

lg.l: Soundre de pors, soundre de esturneus,
Pype des oyseues·, biuebivé des heyrouns,
Greyle de geleyns, turbe de cerceyles·,
Eschele de batayle, lure de pucel.
Le boefe mugiste,. le A gap has been left here for the missing word. groule,

body.note: A line appears to be missing at the top of this column due to page trimming, which would correspond to T 218: Et homme e beste sovent abaye

lg.l: Le porke groundile, la gelyn patile,
Pounte en gardine, pounte en Vilevile.
En Fraunce est trouevé cest estile·
Que gelyn hupehupé pounte e patile.
Qui plus patile pur vnun oefe·
Que pur la arrure ne feste le boefe.
Espicer prente ses merces de bale·.
A soen seriauntjaunte sa chose bayle·.
Aprés dormire il se espreche. raxels
Non pas vus mes toun aver

body.note: A line appears to be missing at the top of the column, corresponding to T 251: Ke soune tut un [en] engleis.

lg.l: Pur vnun Reygnereygne asser rey floke
En vnun reoun par sey·. fur·
Luy lere qui vint la foreier.
Od fauce fachez vnun In the context of manuscript damage it is difficult to be certain, but it appears that the relevant noun has been omitted from the text and a gap has been left here.
Dehors la grange vos blez tassez
There is a change of ink from this line.vostre ble est batu·
le paste al auge a aherd
Du peel mettez le pain en four·,
E taunt com fet laa soioursojour
Nota in the margin next to this line, marking the start of the new section.Aletz, dame messnere·,
E autre foitz le lin elisez.
E ieojeo vus pri, dameDame murMuriel,
E blee est molu par fusil.
One line cut off at the top of the page by trimming, equivalent to MS T line 347: A wuderes hore aleez.E vostre filee la voidez·.
Mes seoms ore en tour cerueiseveise·
Pur fere le gente bien aese·.
Alumez, auncele, la frenole·. þe kex
Taunt qe seit bien germegermé. becoomn
Le brees de vostre meine mohez·
En mouncels ou en rengez·, heppes rawes
Le corbail &e le corbeiloun lep skep
One line cut off at the top of the page due to trimming, corresponding to T 380: Kaunt vostre brés est molu.E de ewe chaude bien
De home lei fet bon clerk.
Taunt de vertue ad la grees
Qe la coif de vnun bricoun
Nota in the margin next to this line, marking the start of the new section.Ore voloms pescher
De la raiz en vnun viueviver.
me entre vnun anf de neif,

l.foreign: redRed þis redel, wat it may be

lg.l: Qauunt les daies ne poespoés pas·

l.said: Jeo ai la maine si estomie·
Qe pur freid ne puis ieojeo mie·

lg.l: Dount meulz vusrei sur plume mole
Seer pres du fu qe mener karole
Pur fere chapels a clers dd'escole
Qe par boste de friuolefrivole· ydel

&E par kniuetknivet od virole·A line appears to be missing between 448 and 449, corresponding to T 467, which would complete the construction of which qe par boste de frivole... is a sub-clause. Cf. T 467: Sevent attrere femme fole.
Amour de femme est si mole,
Plust a deuDeu qe teles foles·
VssentUssent face pleine de veroles poks
Les nouns dedé quels cy vus deuynedevyne·.
La floure de rose, la flure dede litz·:
Liz vaut pur roigne, rose pur pitz·.
Cy crest la flure de sourcye·
Qe vers les sourcyes ad nule enuyeenvye·.
Li oil quiqui est pres du sourciz
De vere la sourcie ad grant delitz·.
Sur tere pergent en tens de veire·.
E bugle ausi, vneune herbe sein·.

l.gloss: gletThere is currently no MED entry for this form, though it must be related to attested forms like clete, clote. The trilingual herbal glossary in Oxford, Bodley MS 761 includes the forms gletonere and glote; the former looks French while the latter may be English.·

lg.l: E cholet plauntez en lour lu·.
En ceo verger les arbres cressent·.
Par charche dede fruit les vnesunes abessent·.
en auncestrie alThe referent and sense of the gloss is unclear: is it glossing auncestrie, or porte or alies on the preceding line?
Le noun receut par vnun rei·
A line across the column appears to suggest a missing line in the right margin, lost to page trimming, which would correspond to T 522: E pur ceo ke seyngnour fet coynner.Son neif en ceppes pur

Qe de nuitMost versions of the text have a negative here, which would render the line (referring to holly) as: which is of no value for a blanket at night. The lack of negation appears to create a non-sequiter in this version, unless de nuit is understood as a source of harm (from nuisir) or read as nint, expressing the negation not. vaut pur

A nota line across the column may be indicating an omitted couplet (present in other manuscripts, eg. T 534-535): De suheus l'em fount suheus / Un manger ke est bon e beus. It is impossible to be certain due to the trimming of the right margin.De sauce &e chene &e del

Most of the material in the remainder of the Trees section (not present in other surviving Tretiz MSS) is also found in the Anglo-French Geste de Blancheflour et de Florence, lines 52–72, which I have used to supply line endings where these can be ascertained with a high degree of probability.Arbres sount des autres partz
Ou la grape est sour la vigne:
WidescheneAND has this as vi de chesne, with the sense of mistletoe, attested from the Geste de Blancheflour et de Florance, line 59. e popeler,
CylPossibly to be identified with AND entry siler (also listing variant form silhe: unidentified tree or shrub, possibly a kind of willow. pin &e neffler

A line has been cut off at the top of the page, corresponding to MS T 545: Regardez cy, Alysaundre, (the bottom part of the word Alisaundre is just visible at the very top of the remaining page).apeleapelé alisaundre·.

de frestele.A line across the column below this line leads to the marginal annotation lever it is, which presumably refers to the rhymed English translation found in certain manuscripts, including MS T: Levere is þe wrenne / about þe stouk renne / þen the fiþel dravt / hor þe floute craft. The abbreviated form found in this manuscript suggests that the scribe expected the text's reader to recognise the reference and supply the full version themselves.·
sount les veneles·. The marginal annotation mores may mark a missing line. Compare T 582-583: En mores sount les vaneles / En viles sount les veneles.
Meuth serrey de vnun blaret pu·
Que ne serrei de chare de fru.
Qe nul cigoyn en ceo mound·.

Another horizontal line across the column may indicate a couplet missing just before this line, corresponding to MS T 592-593: E severounder a la severounde / Prent les moussrons a la rounde. Are the letters E s, corresponding to the opening of the couplet, visible in the right margin alongside the ruled line?Wne oysel qe est dite bekace·
Prees de riuererivere est pris en lace.
Il me semble qe le kukuel·

As with the Trees section, the following lines have close parallels with the bird names listed in the Anglo-French Geste de Blancheflour e de Florence, in this case lines 76–90.Aucie, prael &e mortun·,
Pur me dedure en ceo bois·.
Seit en vile seit en bois·,
Force ne fet mes kk'il eit·
Geline ou poucyn a son reheit.
De faere ma grange de ratz nete
De touz les taupes qe sont trové
En terre fowaunt parmy les prez
Qe melodie de ane ou poun. asse· pacok
Mes de poun la bele cowe·The text breaks off at this point.
